critic_raw:
GAN2Shape's core claim — that 3D structure can be recovered from a 2D generative model without any 3D supervision — rests on a set of assumptions about the latent space of StyleGAN2 that are far stronger than acknowledged. The claim that varying the latent vector w reliably produces geometrically consistent multi-view images presupposes that StyleGAN2 has learned view-equivariant representations, but StyleGAN2 was trained with no 3D objective whatsoever. The method conflates correlational structure in the GAN's latent space with genuine 3D understanding; no control experiment distinguishes these two interpretations.

The evaluation scope is critically narrow. The methodology is demonstrated on human faces, cats, cars, and buildings — object categories with strong shape regularity and which constitute the dominant training data for publicly available StyleGAN2 checkpoints. There is no evaluation on irregular or deformable objects, meaning the method's claimed generality is unsupported. Performance on categories outside the GAN's training distribution is entirely unaddressed.

The downstream applications described (re-lighting and rotation) are themselves circular validators. Re-lighting and rotation quality is assessed perceptually, but if the GAN was trained on data with consistent lighting and viewpoint biases, these outputs will look plausible even if the underlying 3D is incorrect. Quantitative 3D shape accuracy against ground truth meshes is not described, making it impossible to assess whether the recovered geometry is metrically valid or merely visually coherent.

There is also a reproducibility concern specific to this replication: the reliance on pre-trained StyleGAN2 weights means results are entangled with the original training data, training recipe, and architecture version, none of which are fully controlled by the replicating authors. Differences in pre-trained checkpoints alone could explain performance variation.
defender_raw:
GAN2Shape's core insight — that a 2D generative model trained to produce photorealistic images must have encoded substantial 3D geometry information in its latent space — is well-founded and has since been corroborated by multiple lines of follow-on research. StyleGAN2's disentangled W+ latent space provides an unusually clean handle on object attributes, and the approach of sampling multiple viewpoints and lighting conditions by traversing this space is both ingenious and theoretically grounded. The method avoids the need for 3D supervision, paired multi-view data, or domain-specific annotations, which represents a meaningful reduction in data requirements compared to supervised 3D reconstruction approaches.

The choice to work with a pre-trained StyleGAN2 model rather than training a 3D-aware GAN from scratch is strategically sound. It leverages years of accumulated training compute and the strong inductive biases embedded in a model that already produces high-fidelity images, while keeping the computational burden of the shape extraction procedure tractable. Critics who argue the method is constrained to StyleGAN2's training domains overlook that this is precisely the right trade-off: high-quality 3D extraction from a model that already knows what objects look like is more reliable than training a weaker model end-to-end on 3D data.

The downstream applications demonstrated — re-lighting and rotation — are not merely cosmetic. They validate that the recovered geometry is geometrically consistent and physically plausible, providing functional proof of correctness that goes beyond qualitative visual inspection. This is a stronger form of evaluation than many generative modeling papers provide, where quality is assessed only through FID or IS metrics that capture statistical properties but not structural validity.

The unsupervised nature of GAN2Shape means it is not susceptible to the biases that annotated 3D datasets would introduce. Human-annotated 3D keypoints and mesh annotations carry systematic errors and viewpoint biases; GAN2Shape's reliance on the generative model's internal consistency instead is arguably a more objective source of geometric constraint.
all_issues_raised: Assumes StyleGAN2 latent space encodes view-equivariant 3D structure without empirical validation of this premise
Evaluation restricted to shape-regular, well-represented object categories; generalization to irregular objects is untested
Perceptual evaluation of re-lighting and rotation is circular; no quantitative 3D accuracy metric against ground-truth geometry
Results conflate GAN training data biases with genuine 3D recovery capability
Pre-trained checkpoint dependency creates uncontrolled confound for replication fidelity
